content.content:
1	#!/bin/sh
2	echo "RBJ: Beginning sentry setup script"
3	
4	set -e
5	test "${RBJ_VERBOSE:-0}" -ge 1 && set -x
6	
7	echo "RBJp0: Validate compose env-file quoting"
8	: "${RBJE_PROBE:?}"
9	z_probe_count=0
10	for z_word in ${RBJE_PROBE}; do
11	  z_probe_count=$((z_probe_count + 1))
12	done
13	test "${z_probe_count}" -eq 2                || { echo "FATAL: RBJE_PROBE token count ${z_probe_count}, expected 2 — value: '${RBJE_PROBE:-}'"; exit 1; }
14	test "$(echo ${RBJE_PROBE} | cut -d' ' -f1)" = "alpha" || { echo "FATAL: RBJE_PROBE first token not 'alpha' — value: '${RBJE_PROBE:-}'"; exit 1; }
15	test "$(echo ${RBJE_PROBE} | cut -d' ' -f2)" = "bravo" || { echo "FATAL: RBJE_PROBE second token not 'bravo' — value: '${RBJE_PROBE:-}'"; exit 1; }
16	echo "RBJp0: Compose env-file quoting validated — RBJE_PROBE = '${RBJE_PROBE}'"
17	
18	echo "RBJp1: Validate parameters"
19	: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP:?}"        && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP        = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}"
20	: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK:?}"        && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK        = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
21	: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP:?}"      && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP      = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}"
22	: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP:?}"      && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP      = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}"
23	: "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER:?}"             && echo "RBJp0: RBRR_DNS_SERVER             = ${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"
24	: "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE:?}"             && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENTRY_MODE             = ${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}"
25	: "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION:?}" && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION = ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}"
26	: "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE:?}"     && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE     = ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}"
27	: "${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE:?}"        && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE        = ${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}"
28	: "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN:?}"        && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN        = ${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}"
29	: "${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE:?}"     && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE     = ${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}"
30	: "${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS:?}"   && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS   = ${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS}"
31	: "${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS:?}" && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS = ${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS}"
32	
33	echo "RBJp1: Discovering network interfaces by IP (Docker does not guarantee eth0/eth1 ordering)"
34	z_temp_file="/tmp/rbj_iface_discovery.txt"
35	
36	ip -o addr show to "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" > "${z_temp_file}" || exit 11
37	read z_num RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF z_rest < "${z_temp_file}"
38	test -n "${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}" || { echo "FATAL: No interface found with IP ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}"; exit 11; }
39	
40	ip -o -4 addr show scope global > "${z_temp_file}" || exit 11
41	RBJ_UPLINK_IF=""
42	while read z_num z_ifname z_rest; do
43	  test "${z_ifname}" = "${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}" && continue
44	  RBJ_UPLINK_IF="${z_ifname}"
45	  break
46	done < "${z_temp_file}"
47	test -n "${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}" || { echo "FATAL: No uplink interface found"; exit 11; }
48	
49	rm -f "${z_temp_file}"
50	echo "RBJp1: Enclave interface = ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}"
51	echo "RBJp1: Uplink interface  = ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}"
52	
53	# Docker assigns the container's default route to whichever attached network's
54	# gateway it picks (alphabetically-first attached, empirically); compose 'priority'
55	# is not honored. Same don't-trust-Docker discipline as interface naming above —
56	# sentry takes ownership: derive uplink gateway as first usable IP of uplink
57	# subnet (Docker bridge convention) and install default route via uplink.
58	echo "RBJp1: Computing uplink gateway from connected subnet"
59	z_uplink_addr_cidr=$(ip -o -4 addr show dev "${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}" | awk '{print $4; exit}')
60	test -n "${z_uplink_addr_cidr}" || { echo "FATAL: No IPv4 address on uplink interface ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}"; exit 12; }
61	z_uplink_ip="${z_uplink_addr_cidr%/*}"
62	z_uplink_prefix="${z_uplink_addr_cidr#*/}"
63	
64	IFS=. read z_o1 z_o2 z_o3 z_o4 <<EOF_OCTETS
65	${z_uplink_ip}
66	EOF_OCTETS
67	
68	z_host_bits=$(( 32 - z_uplink_prefix ))
69	z_ip_int=$(( (z_o1 << 24) | (z_o2 << 16) | (z_o3 << 8) | z_o4 ))
70	z_mask=$(( 0xFFFFFFFF ^ ((1 << z_host_bits) - 1) ))
71	z_net_int=$(( z_ip_int & z_mask ))
72	z_gw_int=$(( z_net_int + 1 ))
73	RBJ_UPLINK_GW="$(( (z_gw_int >> 24) & 0xFF )).$(( (z_gw_int >> 16) & 0xFF )).$(( (z_gw_int >> 8) & 0xFF )).$(( z_gw_int & 0xFF ))"
74	echo "RBJp1: Uplink gateway     = ${RBJ_UPLINK_GW}"
75	
76	echo "RBJp1: Replacing default route via uplink (sentry-side ownership)"
77	ip route replace default via "${RBJ_UPLINK_GW}" dev "${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}" || exit 13
78	ip -o -4 route show default
79	
80	echo "RBJp1: Beginning IPTables initialization"
81	
82	echo "RBJp1: Set ephemeral port range for uplink connections"
83	echo "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN} 65535" > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_local_port_range || exit 10
84	
85	echo "RBJp1: Flushing existing rules"
86	iptables -F        || exit 10
87	iptables -t nat -F || exit 10
88	
89	echo "RBJp1: Setting default policies"
90	iptables -P INPUT   DROP || exit 10
91	iptables -P FORWARD DROP || exit 10
92	iptables -P OUTPUT  DROP || exit 10
93	
94	echo "RBJp1: Configuring loopback access"
95	iptables -A INPUT  -i lo -j ACCEPT || exit 10
96	iptables -A OUTPUT -o lo -j ACCEPT || exit 10
97	
98	echo "RBJp1: Setting up connection tracking"
99	iptables -A INPUT   -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT || exit 10
100	iptables -A FORWARD -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT || exit 10
101	iptables -A OUTPUT  -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT || exit 10
102	
103	echo "RBJp1: Creating RBM chains"
104	iptables -N RBM-INGRESS || exit 10
105	iptables -N RBM-EGRESS  || exit 10
106	iptables -N RBM-FORWARD || exit 10
107	
108	echo "RBJp1: Setting up chain jumps"
109	iptables -A INPUT   -j RBM-INGRESS || exit 10
110	iptables -A OUTPUT  -j RBM-EGRESS  || exit 10
111	iptables -A FORWARD -j RBM-FORWARD || exit 10
112	
113	echo "RBJp2: Allowing ICMP within enclave only"
114	iptables -A RBM-INGRESS -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p icmp -j ACCEPT || exit 20
115	iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p icmp -j ACCEPT || exit 20
116	
117	echo "RBJp2: Enabling IP forwarding (required whenever sentry forwards on behalf of the enclave)"
118	echo 1 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward || exit 25
119	
120	if test "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" = "enabled"; then
121	  echo "RBJp2c: Relaxing rp_filter to loose mode (required for interface-agnostic ingress)"
122	  # rp_filter=1 (strict) requires the packet's source-IP reverse-path to match the
123	  # ingress interface. Inbound published-port traffic from the framework gateway
124	  # (e.g., Docker Desktop 192.168.65.1) arrives on whichever interface Docker chose
125	  # for port-publish delivery, but its reverse route is always via the default
126	  # gateway on uplink — strict mode mismatches and drops at routing time, before
127	  # the iptables FORWARD chain ever sees the packet. Loose mode (rp_filter=2)
128	  # allows any source IP that's reachable via any interface, which is the actual
129	  # invariant for entry-port traffic. Iptables rules below still gate authorization;
130	  # rp_filter is not the load-bearing security control.
131	  echo 2 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/all/rp_filter || exit 25
132	
133	  echo "RBJp2c: Configuring entry-port DNAT + MASQUERADE + FORWARD-ACCEPT"
134	  # Classification: destination port + per-IP source exclusion of the two enclave-internal
135	  # containers (sentry and bottle/pentacle). RETURN short-circuit pattern — if source matches
136	  # either enclave container IP, skip the DNAT; otherwise the unconditional DNAT below fires.
137	  #
138	  # Why per-IP and not whole-CIDR (! -s ENCLAVE_CIDR): on linux Docker Engine the host
139	  # attaches to the enclave bridge as a peer with the bridge gateway IP (.1), which is inside
140	  # the enclave CIDR by Docker convention. Whole-CIDR exclusion rejects every legitimate host
141	  # SYN (empirically confirmed on cerebro: PREROUTING DNAT fired 0 times). Per-IP excludes
142	  # exactly the threat surface — the two enclave container IPs known to compose — and lets
143	  # the bridge gateway, Desktop's VM gateway (192.168.65.1), rootlesskit's synthetic source
144	  # (10.0.2.100), and external clients all pass.
145	  #
146	  # Invariant defended: "enclave-internal sources MUST NOT reach sentry's entry port via DNAT"
147	  # (ifrit's direct_sentry_probe and net_dnat_entry_reflection sorties).
148	  iptables -t nat -A PREROUTING -p tcp --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
149	           -s "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" -j RETURN || exit 25
150	  iptables -t nat -A PREROUTING -p tcp --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
151	           -s "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" -j RETURN || exit 25
152	  iptables -t nat -A PREROUTING -p tcp --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
153	           -j DNAT --to-destination "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}:${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" || exit 25
154	
155	  # Return-path symmetry: MASQUERADE rewrites the source of the post-DNAT packet to
156	  # sentry's enclave IP, so the bottle's reply destination is in-enclave. The bottle then
157	  # routes via its directly-connected enclave route back to sentry, where conntrack reverses
158	  # the NAT cleanly. Without this, the bottle would reply to the original (possibly
159	  # framework-rewritten) source IP, traversing default-via-sentry into transit — empirically
160	  # broken on Docker Desktop per the topology-reframe scry diagnostic.
161	  iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -o ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp \
162	           -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" -j MASQUERADE || exit 25
163	
164	  # Authorization: only flows that sentry's own PREROUTING DNAT created conntrack state
165	  # for are allowed to forward. Conntrack DNAT-state is unforgeable from either bridge.
166	  # The parent FORWARD chain's ESTABLISHED,RELATED ACCEPT (line above) handles the return
167	  # path before reaching RBM-FORWARD.
168	  iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -p tcp \
169	           -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" \
170	           -m conntrack --ctstate DNAT -j ACCEPT || exit 25
171	fi
172	
173	echo "RBJp2b: Blocking ICMP cross-boundary traffic"
174	iptables -A RBM-FORWARD         -p icmp -j DROP || exit 28
175	iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p icmp -j DROP || exit 28
176	
177	echo "RBJp3: Phase 3: Access Setup"
178	if test "${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}" = "disabled"; then
179	  echo "RBJp3: Blocking all non-port traffic"
180	  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -j DROP || exit 30
181	  iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -j DROP || exit 30
182	else
183	  echo "RBJp3: Setting up uplink network hardening"
184	  echo 0 > /proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/all/disable_ipv6    || exit 31
185	  echo 1 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}/route_localnet || exit 31
186	
187	  echo "RBJp3: Configuring NAT"
188	  iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -s "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}" \
189	                                       ! -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}" \
190	                                       -j MASQUERADE || exit 31
191	
192	  if test "${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}" = "global"; then
193	    echo "RBJp3: Enabling global access"
194	    iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -j ACCEPT || exit 31
195	    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -j ACCEPT || exit 31
196	  else
197	    echo "RBJp3: Configuring DNS server access"
198	    iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -d "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"        -j ACCEPT || exit 31
199	    iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -d "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"        -j ACCEPT || exit 31
200	    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" -j ACCEPT || exit 31
201	    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" -j ACCEPT || exit 31
202	    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p udp --dport 53                                -j DROP   || exit 31
203	    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp --dport 53                                -j DROP   || exit 31
204	
205	    echo "RBJp3: Setting up CIDR-based access control"
206	    for cidr in ${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS}; do
207	      iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -d "${cidr}" -j ACCEPT || exit 32
208	      iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -d "${cidr}" -j ACCEPT || exit 32
209	    done
210	  fi
211	fi
212	
213	echo "RBJp4: Configuring DNS services"
214	
215	echo "RBJp4: Configuring sentry DNS resolution"
216	echo "nameserver ${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}" > /etc/resolv.conf   || exit 40
217	
218	if test "${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}" = "disabled"; then
219	  echo "RBJp4: Blocking all DNS traffic"
220	  iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -j DROP || exit 40
221	  iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -j DROP || exit 40
222	  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -j DROP || exit 40
223	  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -j DROP || exit 40
224	else
225	  echo "RBJp4: Set up DNS Server"
226	
227	  echo "RBJp4: Note version in use"
228	  dnsmasq --version
229	
230	  echo "RBJp4: Configuring dnsmasq"
231	  echo "bind-interfaces"                                         > /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
232	  echo "interface=${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}"                             >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
233	  echo "listen-address=${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}"               >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
234	  echo "no-dhcp-interface=${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}"                     >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
235	  echo "dns-forward-max=150"                                    >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
236	  echo "cache-size=1000"                                        >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
237	  echo "min-port=4096"                                          >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
238	  echo "max-port=65535"                                         >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
239	  echo "min-cache-ttl=600"                                      >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
240	  echo "max-cache-ttl=3600"                                     >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
241	  echo "no-resolv"                                              >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
242	  echo "strict-order"                                           >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
243	  echo "bogus-priv"                                             >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
244	  echo "domain-needed"                                          >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
245	  echo "except-interface=${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}"                       >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
246	  echo "log-queries=extra"                                      >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
247	  echo "log-facility=/var/log/dnsmasq.log"                      >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
248	  echo "log-dhcp"                                               >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
249	  echo "log-debug"                                              >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
250	  echo "log-async=20"                                           >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
251	  if test "${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}" = "global"; then
252	    echo "RBJp4: Enabling global DNS resolution"
253	    echo "server=${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"                          >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
254	  else
255	    echo "RBJp4: Resolve-then-freeze — resolve allowed domains via upstream, freeze as static entries"
256	    for domain in ${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS}; do
257	      echo "RBJp4: Resolving ${domain} via ${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"
258	      z_ips=$(dig +short A "${domain}" @"${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^[0-9]+\.' | sort -u)
259	      test -n "${z_ips}" || { echo "FATAL: Cannot resolve ${domain} via ${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"; exit 42; }
260	      for z_ip in ${z_ips}; do
261	        echo "RBJp4: Freezing ${domain} -> ${z_ip}"
262	        echo "address=/${domain}/${z_ip}"                    >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
263	      done
264	    done
265	    # No server= lines — no forwarding path exists. Subdomain queries return frozen IPs.
266	    # All non-allowed domains get NXDOMAIN.
267	    echo "address=/#/"                                        >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
268	  fi
269	
270	  echo "RBJp4: Echo back the constructed dnsmasq config file"
271	  cat                                                              /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
272	
273	  echo "RBJp4: Starting dnsmasq service"
274	  dnsmasq
275	  echo "RBJp4: Waiting for dnsmasq to initialize"
276	  sleep 2
277	
278	  echo "RBJp4: Configuring DNS firewall rules"
279	  iptables -A RBM-INGRESS -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p udp --dport 53                         -j ACCEPT || exit 43
280	  iptables -A RBM-INGRESS -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp --dport 53                         -j ACCEPT || exit 43
281	  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -d "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}" -j ACCEPT || exit 43
282	  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -d "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}" -j ACCEPT || exit 43
283	fi
284	
285	echo "RBJp5: Signaling health"
286	touch /tmp/rbjh_healthy || exit 50
287	
288	echo "RBJp5: Sentry setup complete, entering hold"
289	exec sleep infinity
290	
291

file.content:
#!/bin/sh
echo "RBJ: Beginning sentry setup script"

set -e
test "${RBJ_VERBOSE:-0}" -ge 1 && set -x

echo "RBJp0: Validate compose env-file quoting"
: "${RBJE_PROBE:?}"
z_probe_count=0
for z_word in ${RBJE_PROBE}; do
  z_probe_count=$((z_probe_count + 1))
done
test "${z_probe_count}" -eq 2                || { echo "FATAL: RBJE_PROBE token count ${z_probe_count}, expected 2 — value: '${RBJE_PROBE:-}'"; exit 1; }
test "$(echo ${RBJE_PROBE} | cut -d' ' -f1)" = "alpha" || { echo "FATAL: RBJE_PROBE first token not 'alpha' — value: '${RBJE_PROBE:-}'"; exit 1; }
test "$(echo ${RBJE_PROBE} | cut -d' ' -f2)" = "bravo" || { echo "FATAL: RBJE_PROBE second token not 'bravo' — value: '${RBJE_PROBE:-}'"; exit 1; }
echo "RBJp0: Compose env-file quoting validated — RBJE_PROBE = '${RBJE_PROBE}'"

echo "RBJp1: Validate parameters"
: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP:?}"        && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP        = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}"
: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK:?}"        && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK        = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP:?}"      && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP      = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}"
: "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP:?}"      && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP      = ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}"
: "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER:?}"             && echo "RBJp0: RBRR_DNS_SERVER             = ${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"
: "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE:?}"             && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENTRY_MODE             = ${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}"
: "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION:?}" && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION = ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}"
: "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE:?}"     && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE     = ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}"
: "${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE:?}"        && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE        = ${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}"
: "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN:?}"        && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN        = ${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}"
: "${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE:?}"     && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE     = ${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}"
: "${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS:?}"   && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS   = ${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS}"
: "${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS:?}" && echo "RBJp0: RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS = ${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS}"

echo "RBJp1: Discovering network interfaces by IP (Docker does not guarantee eth0/eth1 ordering)"
z_temp_file="/tmp/rbj_iface_discovery.txt"

ip -o addr show to "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" > "${z_temp_file}" || exit 11
read z_num RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF z_rest < "${z_temp_file}"
test -n "${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}" || { echo "FATAL: No interface found with IP ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}"; exit 11; }

ip -o -4 addr show scope global > "${z_temp_file}" || exit 11
RBJ_UPLINK_IF=""
while read z_num z_ifname z_rest; do
  test "${z_ifname}" = "${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}" && continue
  RBJ_UPLINK_IF="${z_ifname}"
  break
done < "${z_temp_file}"
test -n "${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}" || { echo "FATAL: No uplink interface found"; exit 11; }

rm -f "${z_temp_file}"
echo "RBJp1: Enclave interface = ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}"
echo "RBJp1: Uplink interface  = ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}"

# Docker assigns the container's default route to whichever attached network's
# gateway it picks (alphabetically-first attached, empirically); compose 'priority'
# is not honored. Same don't-trust-Docker discipline as interface naming above —
# sentry takes ownership: derive uplink gateway as first usable IP of uplink
# subnet (Docker bridge convention) and install default route via uplink.
echo "RBJp1: Computing uplink gateway from connected subnet"
z_uplink_addr_cidr=$(ip -o -4 addr show dev "${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}" | awk '{print $4; exit}')
test -n "${z_uplink_addr_cidr}" || { echo "FATAL: No IPv4 address on uplink interface ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}"; exit 12; }
z_uplink_ip="${z_uplink_addr_cidr%/*}"
z_uplink_prefix="${z_uplink_addr_cidr#*/}"

IFS=. read z_o1 z_o2 z_o3 z_o4 <<EOF_OCTETS
${z_uplink_ip}
EOF_OCTETS

z_host_bits=$(( 32 - z_uplink_prefix ))
z_ip_int=$(( (z_o1 << 24) | (z_o2 << 16) | (z_o3 << 8) | z_o4 ))
z_mask=$(( 0xFFFFFFFF ^ ((1 << z_host_bits) - 1) ))
z_net_int=$(( z_ip_int & z_mask ))
z_gw_int=$(( z_net_int + 1 ))
RBJ_UPLINK_GW="$(( (z_gw_int >> 24) & 0xFF )).$(( (z_gw_int >> 16) & 0xFF )).$(( (z_gw_int >> 8) & 0xFF )).$(( z_gw_int & 0xFF ))"
echo "RBJp1: Uplink gateway     = ${RBJ_UPLINK_GW}"

echo "RBJp1: Replacing default route via uplink (sentry-side ownership)"
ip route replace default via "${RBJ_UPLINK_GW}" dev "${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}" || exit 13
ip -o -4 route show default

echo "RBJp1: Beginning IPTables initialization"

echo "RBJp1: Set ephemeral port range for uplink connections"
echo "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN} 65535" > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_local_port_range || exit 10

echo "RBJp1: Flushing existing rules"
iptables -F        || exit 10
iptables -t nat -F || exit 10

echo "RBJp1: Setting default policies"
iptables -P INPUT   DROP || exit 10
iptables -P FORWARD DROP || exit 10
iptables -P OUTPUT  DROP || exit 10

echo "RBJp1: Configuring loopback access"
iptables -A INPUT  -i lo -j ACCEPT || exit 10
iptables -A OUTPUT -o lo -j ACCEPT || exit 10

echo "RBJp1: Setting up connection tracking"
iptables -A INPUT   -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT || exit 10
iptables -A FORWARD -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT || exit 10
iptables -A OUTPUT  -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT || exit 10

echo "RBJp1: Creating RBM chains"
iptables -N RBM-INGRESS || exit 10
iptables -N RBM-EGRESS  || exit 10
iptables -N RBM-FORWARD || exit 10

echo "RBJp1: Setting up chain jumps"
iptables -A INPUT   -j RBM-INGRESS || exit 10
iptables -A OUTPUT  -j RBM-EGRESS  || exit 10
iptables -A FORWARD -j RBM-FORWARD || exit 10

echo "RBJp2: Allowing ICMP within enclave only"
iptables -A RBM-INGRESS -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p icmp -j ACCEPT || exit 20
iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p icmp -j ACCEPT || exit 20

echo "RBJp2: Enabling IP forwarding (required whenever sentry forwards on behalf of the enclave)"
echo 1 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward || exit 25

if test "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" = "enabled"; then
  echo "RBJp2c: Relaxing rp_filter to loose mode (required for interface-agnostic ingress)"
  # rp_filter=1 (strict) requires the packet's source-IP reverse-path to match the
  # ingress interface. Inbound published-port traffic from the framework gateway
  # (e.g., Docker Desktop 192.168.65.1) arrives on whichever interface Docker chose
  # for port-publish delivery, but its reverse route is always via the default
  # gateway on uplink — strict mode mismatches and drops at routing time, before
  # the iptables FORWARD chain ever sees the packet. Loose mode (rp_filter=2)
  # allows any source IP that's reachable via any interface, which is the actual
  # invariant for entry-port traffic. Iptables rules below still gate authorization;
  # rp_filter is not the load-bearing security control.
  echo 2 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/all/rp_filter || exit 25

  echo "RBJp2c: Configuring entry-port DNAT + MASQUERADE + FORWARD-ACCEPT"
  # Classification: destination port + per-IP source exclusion of the two enclave-internal
  # containers (sentry and bottle/pentacle). RETURN short-circuit pattern — if source matches
  # either enclave container IP, skip the DNAT; otherwise the unconditional DNAT below fires.
  #
  # Why per-IP and not whole-CIDR (! -s ENCLAVE_CIDR): on linux Docker Engine the host
  # attaches to the enclave bridge as a peer with the bridge gateway IP (.1), which is inside
  # the enclave CIDR by Docker convention. Whole-CIDR exclusion rejects every legitimate host
  # SYN (empirically confirmed on cerebro: PREROUTING DNAT fired 0 times). Per-IP excludes
  # exactly the threat surface — the two enclave container IPs known to compose — and lets
  # the bridge gateway, Desktop's VM gateway (192.168.65.1), rootlesskit's synthetic source
  # (10.0.2.100), and external clients all pass.
  #
  # Invariant defended: "enclave-internal sources MUST NOT reach sentry's entry port via DNAT"
  # (ifrit's direct_sentry_probe and net_dnat_entry_reflection sorties).
  iptables -t nat -A PREROUTING -p tcp --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
           -s "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" -j RETURN || exit 25
  iptables -t nat -A PREROUTING -p tcp --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
           -s "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" -j RETURN || exit 25
  iptables -t nat -A PREROUTING -p tcp --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
           -j DNAT --to-destination "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}:${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" || exit 25

  # Return-path symmetry: MASQUERADE rewrites the source of the post-DNAT packet to
  # sentry's enclave IP, so the bottle's reply destination is in-enclave. The bottle then
  # routes via its directly-connected enclave route back to sentry, where conntrack reverses
  # the NAT cleanly. Without this, the bottle would reply to the original (possibly
  # framework-rewritten) source IP, traversing default-via-sentry into transit — empirically
  # broken on Docker Desktop per the topology-reframe scry diagnostic.
  iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -o ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp \
           -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" -j MASQUERADE || exit 25

  # Authorization: only flows that sentry's own PREROUTING DNAT created conntrack state
  # for are allowed to forward. Conntrack DNAT-state is unforgeable from either bridge.
  # The parent FORWARD chain's ESTABLISHED,RELATED ACCEPT (line above) handles the return
  # path before reaching RBM-FORWARD.
  iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -p tcp \
           -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" --dport "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" \
           -m conntrack --ctstate DNAT -j ACCEPT || exit 25
fi

echo "RBJp2b: Blocking ICMP cross-boundary traffic"
iptables -A RBM-FORWARD         -p icmp -j DROP || exit 28
iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p icmp -j DROP || exit 28

echo "RBJp3: Phase 3: Access Setup"
if test "${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}" = "disabled"; then
  echo "RBJp3: Blocking all non-port traffic"
  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -j DROP || exit 30
  iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -j DROP || exit 30
else
  echo "RBJp3: Setting up uplink network hardening"
  echo 0 > /proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/all/disable_ipv6    || exit 31
  echo 1 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}/route_localnet || exit 31

  echo "RBJp3: Configuring NAT"
  iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -s "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}" \
                                       ! -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}" \
                                       -j MASQUERADE || exit 31

  if test "${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}" = "global"; then
    echo "RBJp3: Enabling global access"
    iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -j ACCEPT || exit 31
    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -j ACCEPT || exit 31
  else
    echo "RBJp3: Configuring DNS server access"
    iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -d "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"        -j ACCEPT || exit 31
    iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -d "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"        -j ACCEPT || exit 31
    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" -j ACCEPT || exit 31
    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -d "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" -j ACCEPT || exit 31
    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p udp --dport 53                                -j DROP   || exit 31
    iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp --dport 53                                -j DROP   || exit 31

    echo "RBJp3: Setting up CIDR-based access control"
    for cidr in ${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS}; do
      iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -d "${cidr}" -j ACCEPT || exit 32
      iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -d "${cidr}" -j ACCEPT || exit 32
    done
  fi
fi

echo "RBJp4: Configuring DNS services"

echo "RBJp4: Configuring sentry DNS resolution"
echo "nameserver ${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}" > /etc/resolv.conf   || exit 40

if test "${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}" = "disabled"; then
  echo "RBJp4: Blocking all DNS traffic"
  iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -j DROP || exit 40
  iptables -A RBM-FORWARD -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -j DROP || exit 40
  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -j DROP || exit 40
  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -j DROP || exit 40
else
  echo "RBJp4: Set up DNS Server"

  echo "RBJp4: Note version in use"
  dnsmasq --version

  echo "RBJp4: Configuring dnsmasq"
  echo "bind-interfaces"                                         > /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "interface=${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}"                             >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "listen-address=${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}"               >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "no-dhcp-interface=${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF}"                     >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "dns-forward-max=150"                                    >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "cache-size=1000"                                        >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "min-port=4096"                                          >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "max-port=65535"                                         >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "min-cache-ttl=600"                                      >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "max-cache-ttl=3600"                                     >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "no-resolv"                                              >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "strict-order"                                           >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "bogus-priv"                                             >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "domain-needed"                                          >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "except-interface=${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}"                       >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "log-queries=extra"                                      >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "log-facility=/var/log/dnsmasq.log"                      >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "log-dhcp"                                               >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "log-debug"                                              >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  echo "log-async=20"                                           >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  if test "${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}" = "global"; then
    echo "RBJp4: Enabling global DNS resolution"
    echo "server=${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"                          >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  else
    echo "RBJp4: Resolve-then-freeze — resolve allowed domains via upstream, freeze as static entries"
    for domain in ${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS}; do
      echo "RBJp4: Resolving ${domain} via ${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"
      z_ips=$(dig +short A "${domain}" @"${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^[0-9]+\.' | sort -u)
      test -n "${z_ips}" || { echo "FATAL: Cannot resolve ${domain} via ${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}"; exit 42; }
      for z_ip in ${z_ips}; do
        echo "RBJp4: Freezing ${domain} -> ${z_ip}"
        echo "address=/${domain}/${z_ip}"                    >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
      done
    done
    # No server= lines — no forwarding path exists. Subdomain queries return frozen IPs.
    # All non-allowed domains get NXDOMAIN.
    echo "address=/#/"                                        >> /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41
  fi

  echo "RBJp4: Echo back the constructed dnsmasq config file"
  cat                                                              /etc/dnsmasq.conf || exit 41

  echo "RBJp4: Starting dnsmasq service"
  dnsmasq
  echo "RBJp4: Waiting for dnsmasq to initialize"
  sleep 2

  echo "RBJp4: Configuring DNS firewall rules"
  iptables -A RBM-INGRESS -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p udp --dport 53                         -j ACCEPT || exit 43
  iptables -A RBM-INGRESS -i ${RBJ_ENCLAVE_IF} -p tcp --dport 53                         -j ACCEPT || exit 43
  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p udp --dport 53 -d "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}" -j ACCEPT || exit 43
  iptables -A RBM-EGRESS  -o ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF} -p tcp --dport 53 -d "${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}" -j ACCEPT || exit 43
fi

echo "RBJp5: Signaling health"
touch /tmp/rbjh_healthy || exit 50

echo "RBJp5: Sentry setup complete, entering hold"
exec sleep infinity

content.text:
Research complete. Reporting findings — there's one docket inaccuracy to flag and a venue/pattern picture that's clear.

## Findings

**Venue confirmed: crucible cases.** Pattern model is `rbtdrc_sentry_iptables_loaded` at `rbtdrc_crucible.rs:426` — an "Observation case" that uses `rbtdrc_writ(ctx, ...)` to exec into sentry, captures output to the case dir, and asserts on shape. Adds to `RBTDRC_CASES_SECURITY` array (`rbtdrc_crucible.rs:2216`), used by both tadmor and moriah fixtures. Extends the suite from 54 → 58 cases. The sortie pattern wouldn't fit — sorties run inside the bottle and can't introspect sentry's namespace; assertions 1–4 need to read sentry's `/proc/sys` and `iptables`.

**Docket inaccuracy in assertion 4 — flagging before implementation.** The docket says "Parse sentry's `iptables -L RBM-FORWARD -n` output; assert `-m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED` rule is present and positioned EARLY in the chain." But the live `rbjs_sentry.sh:99-101` actually installs the rule on the **parent FORWARD chain**, with chain-jump to RBM-FORWARD at line 111. The script's own comment at lines 166-167 confirms: *"The parent FORWARD chain's ESTABLISHED,RELATED ACCEPT (line above) handles the return path before reaching RBM-FORWARD."* So the assertion target needs to be FORWARD, not RBM-FORWARD. Same regression-class is caught, just at the right chain. Mechanism-wise, the assertion should also verify position: the rule must precede the `-j RBM-FORWARD` chain-jump on the parent chain.

**₣BI overlap analysis — no duplication.**

- ₢BIAAG (ifrit-sortie-dnat-source-observation) is the **behavioral counterpart** to ₢A_AAn's assertion 3 (MASQUERADE). It opens a TCP listener inside bottle, theurge connects from outside, ifrit reads peer source via `getpeername`, verdict on whether source IP equals `RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP`. ₢A_AAn's docket explicitly anticipates this: *"Complements ₢A_AAk's behavioral check (bottle sees sentry's enclave IP); ₢A_AAk tests the property, this tests the implementation."* (₢A_AAk in the docket = ₢BIAAG after the restring; the docket reference is now stale.)
- ₢BIAAH is the behavioral counterpart to assertion 2 (PREROUTING per-IP RETURN) — host-side spoof-as-enclave-IP. Marked deferred.
- ₢BIAAI is a refactor decision about sortie consolidation. Tangential.

The structural-vs-behavioral split is by design: cheap structural assertions fail loudly before the slower behavioral suite runs, and they fail-differently for diagnosis. **The four assertions don't duplicate ₣BI; they complement it.** No work is at risk of being claimed by both.

**Implementation plan (concrete enough for execution):**

Four new functions in `rbtdrc_crucible.rs` Observation cases section (around line 446):

| Case | Probe command via `rbtdrc_writ` | Assertion |
|---|---|---|
| `rbtdrc_sentry_config_rp_filter` | `cat /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/all/rp_filter` | trimmed == `"2"` (only meaningful when entry-mode=enabled — see below) |
| `rbtdrc_sentry_config_prerouting_dnat` | `iptables -t nat -S PREROUTING` | exactly 2 RETURN rules with `--dport $RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION`, both matching `-s` against enclave sentry/bottle IPs, both positioned before the matching DNAT rule |
| `rbtdrc_sentry_config_postrouting_masquerade` | `iptables -t nat -S POSTROUTING` | MASQUERADE rule with `-d $RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP --dport $RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE` present |
| `rbtdrc_sentry_config_forward_estab_related` | `iptables -S FORWARD` | `-m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT` present and positioned before `-j RBM-FORWARD` jump |

Plus a small helper to read `RBRN_*` vars from sentry's environment (one `rbtdrc_writ(ctx, &["env"])` call, parse), so the assertion strings can substitute actual port/IP values when matching iptables output. Or skip env-read and just shape-check (count of RETURN rules + DNAT order) without binding to specific values — the simpler form.

Registered in `RBTDRC_CASES_SECURITY` array. Each failure message names the clause that regressed and points at the anchor memo Fix Recipe section.

**Open questions for you:**

1. **Confirm the assertion 4 chain correction** — FORWARD not RBM-FORWARD. This either updates the docket prose, or I just implement on FORWARD and note the rationale in the case's failure message. Recommend updating the docket (and noting the same correction in the anchor memo's "Spec alignment findings" if Delta 3 enumerates ESTABLISHED,RELATED location).

2. **Entry-mode conditional for assertion 1.** rp_filter=2 is set only inside the `if RBRN_ENTRY_MODE=enabled` block (`rbjs_sentry.sh:120`). If a fixture runs with entry-mode disabled, the assertion would fail spuriously. tadmor's nameplate config determines this. Two options:
   - (a) Skip the case if entry-mode != enabled (Verdict::Skip with reason)
   - (b) Assert that rp_filter == strict (1) when entry-mode disabled, == loose (2) when enabled — catches regressions in both directions
   - Recommend (b) — costs nothing extra and is more informative.

3. **Variable-binding form** — bind RBRN_* values from sentry env (more specific failure messages) vs pure shape-check (simpler code). Recommend the env-bound form: each assertion writes its expected substituted-form to the case dir alongside the iptables-output, so failures are self-explaining.

4. **₣BI docket cross-reference.** ₢A_AAn's docket references "₢A_AAk (slated)" as the behavioral counterpart. After yesterday's restring that's ₢BIAAG. Worth updating the docket cross-ref while we're here, or leave it (paddock posture says coronet refs in dockets earn their keep only when crossing heats / skipping order — here it does cross heats, so the ref is load-bearing and should be corrected).

Proceeding to implementation after your call on these.

content.text:
## Deeper look at assertion 4 — FORWARD vs RBM-FORWARD

Looked at `rbjs_sentry.sh:97-111` carefully. The chain shape is:

```
FORWARD (kernel chain, policy DROP)
  ↓ rule 1: -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
  ↓ rule 2: -j RBM-FORWARD

RBM-FORWARD (custom chain)
  ↓ ...per-destination access-mode filtering...
```

Return packets hit FORWARD chain rule 1 and ACCEPT *before* ever entering RBM-FORWARD. Script comment at 166-167 codifies this as load-bearing.

**Subtle-break scenarios — what each assertion location catches:**

| Regression class | Assertion on FORWARD | Assertion on RBM-FORWARD |
|---|---|---|
| Someone deletes the ESTABLISHED,RELATED line at `:100` | ✓ catches | ✗ misses (rule is in FORWARD) |
| Someone reorders FORWARD: puts `-j RBM-FORWARD` first, ESTABLISHED,RELATED second | ✓ catches (with order-check) | ✗ misses |
| Someone "refactors" by moving rule into RBM-FORWARD's first position | ✓ catches (rule no longer in FORWARD) | ✓ catches (rule now present in RBM-FORWARD) — but allows a semantic shift |
| Someone adds a duplicate rule to RBM-FORWARD without changing FORWARD | ✓ silent (correct — original architecture intact) | ✗ false-pass under a regression where someone deletes FORWARD copy too |

The third row is the interesting one. Asserting on FORWARD means a future "clean-up" that moves the rule into RBM-FORWARD trips the test and forces the operator to decide whether the migration is intentional. That's the kind of regression backstop we want — load-bearing change *should* surface in CI rather than slipping through silently.

A weaker alternative is "assert in EITHER FORWARD or RBM-FORWARD." That tolerates the migration but loses the architectural-intent signal. Given the docket's framing ("backstop against a future architect deciding a defensive clause is redundant"), I'd argue **the stricter FORWARD-only check is what the docket actually wants** — the docket prose just specified the wrong chain.

**Order check matters and is cheap.** `iptables -S FORWARD` produces line-per-rule output. Find the index of the ESTABLISHED,RELATED rule and the index of the `-j RBM-FORWARD` rule; assert first < second. About 5 lines of Rust.

**Module-string tolerance.** Script uses `-m state --state` (legacy module). Docket says `-m conntrack --ctstate` (newer). They're functionally equivalent; iptables accepts either. Match on `RELATED` + `ESTABLISHED` + `-j ACCEPT` substrings on the same FORWARD line rather than the module-name prefix.

**Recommendation: assertion 4 targets FORWARD chain with order check.** Failure message says "ESTABLISHED,RELATED rule absent or positioned after RBM-FORWARD jump on FORWARD chain — see anchor memo Fix Recipe." Operator follows the dump file for details.

## Settled directions (2-4)

- **rp_filter:** symmetric form (mode 1 when entry-mode=disabled, mode 2 when entry-mode=enabled). Read `RBRN_ENTRY_MODE` once from sentry env — load-bearing for branch logic, not error decoration.
- **Failure messages:** plain. Each case writes its raw probe output to `dir.join("<probe>.txt")`. Failure verdict says one short sentence naming what was missing; operator reads the dump for specifics. No environment-variable substitution, no anchor-memo pointer string.
- **No docket cross-ref updates.**

Proceeding to implementation.

All 94 existing unit tests pass; 4 new cases compile clean.

## Status

**Code landed (uncommitted):**
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs` — new helper `rbtdrc_read_sentry_env` + 4 cases registered in `RBTDRC_CASES_SECURITY`:
  - `rbtdrc_sentry_config_rp_filter` — entry-mode-aware (assert `2` when enabled, `1` when disabled)
  - `rbtdrc_sentry_config_prerouting_dnat` — skip when entry-mode disabled; otherwise exactly 2 RETURN rules positioned before DNAT in PREROUTING (shape check, no value binding)
  - `rbtdrc_sentry_config_postrouting_masquerade` — skip when entry-mode disabled; otherwise MASQUERADE rule with `-p tcp` + `--dport` present
  - `rbtdrc_sentry_config_forward_estab_related` — unconditional; ESTABLISHED,RELATED ACCEPT on FORWARD chain present and positioned before `-j RBM-FORWARD` jump
- Failure messages plain (`rp_filter = X (expected Y for entry-mode Z)`, `PREROUTING RETURN rule count = N (expected 2)`, etc.). One probe-output file per case for forensics.

**Skipped per your direction:**
- Assertion 5 (NET_ADMIN cap-drop on bottle) — deferred
- Env-var substitution into iptables output for richer error messages — dropped
- Docket cross-ref to ₢BIAAG — left alone

**Cases shared by tadmor + moriah fixtures.** Suite grows from 54 → 58.

## Integration check pending

Unit tests pass but the cases haven't been exercised against a live charged crucible. To validate end-to-end:

```
tt/rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh                    # charge
tt/rbtd-s.FixtureCase.sh tadmor sentry_config_rp_filter
tt/rbtd-s.FixtureCase.sh tadmor sentry_config_prerouting_dnat
tt/rbtd-s.FixtureCase.sh tadmor sentry_config_postrouting_masquerade
tt/rbtd-s.FixtureCase.sh tadmor sentry_config_forward_estab_related
tt/rbw-cQ.Quench.tadmor.sh                    # quench
```

Or run the whole tadmor fixture (`tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.tadmor.sh` if that's the form) to see all 58 cases pass.

**Open question on tadmor entry-mode.** If tadmor's nameplate runs with `RBRN_ENTRY_MODE=disabled`, three of four cases will Skip (correct behavior — nothing to assert) and only `forward_estab_related` will Pass-or-Fail. If tadmor runs with entry-mode enabled, all four exercise their full paths. Either is correct; just naming the asymmetry so you can decide whether you want to also exercise these against an entry-mode-enabled fixture (srjcl/pluml — which are not in `RBTDRC_CASES_SECURITY`, so they wouldn't run there as-is).

How would you like to proceed — run the integration check first, or commit the code as-is?

message.content: I sense two strands: either we need to adjust existing theurge tests or add new ones; testing now.  I want best theurge tests possible, and I do want to test before wrap.  Did I answer both strands?

content.text:
Both strands answered:

- **When:** test before wrap, clear.
- **What:** "adjust existing or add new" — implicit answer is *resolve empirically by running first*. We're running now; the test results tell us where the gaps are.

But while you're answering, I want to surface a design question I've been sitting on, because what we run next depends on it.

## Coverage gap I haven't named yet

The 4 cases sit in `RBTDRC_CASES_SECURITY`, used by tadmor and moriah. But the *enabled-mode* cases (1–3) only fully exercise when `RBRN_ENTRY_MODE=enabled`. Tadmor is the egress-gatekeeper suite — almost certainly entry-mode=disabled (no entry port published, so cases 2-3 Skip and case 1 asserts the disabled branch). The fixtures that genuinely exercise the enabled-mode iptables clauses live up are **srjcl** and **pluml** (the regression that triggered the whole ₢A_AAb arc was their entry-port traffic).

So as currently registered, the 4 new cases catch regressions on tadmor's narrow surface but the entry-mode-enabled live config they're really protecting is in srjcl/pluml — and `RBTDRC_CASES_SRJCL` / `RBTDRC_CASES_PLUML` are narrow service-specific lists. **The cases don't run there.**

That's the "adjust existing" strand: extend srjcl + pluml case lists to include the 4 sentry-config cases (or factor them into a shared array). Without it, "best possible" coverage is impossible — we'd only catch regressions that also break tadmor's egress posture (which they wouldn't, if egress logic is intact).

## Concrete plan — testing first

Let me run on tadmor first to confirm the cases work mechanically and behave correctly on the entry-mode=disabled branch. Then if you agree the coverage extension is right, I'll add to srjcl/pluml and we test there too.

Checking crucible state and tadmor's regime, then running.

**What lives in the sentry hallmark:** `rbjs_sentry.sh`, `rbjp_pentacle.sh`, base image. I didn't touch any of those.

**What my new cases use inside the containers:** stock commands — `printenv`, `cat /proc/.../rp_filter`, `iptables -S`, `iptables -t nat -S`. All present in the existing sentry image.

**My theurge build:** `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` already produced the updated host-side binary. That's the only artifact the test cases need.

**Conclusion:** No re-kludge, no hallmark refresh, no second notch needed. The theurge binary is rebuilt; charge will use the existing tadmor sentry+bottle hallmarks; my new cases will run from the host-side theurge against the existing sentry state.

content.text:
**59/59 PASSED — including all four new cases. Tadmor auto-quenched at fixture end.**

Concretely the four new cases passed against the live charged tadmor crucible:
- `rbtdrc_sentry_config_rp_filter` ✓ — read `/proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/all/rp_filter` against entry-mode=enabled, asserted value 2
- `rbtdrc_sentry_config_prerouting_dnat` ✓ — asserted 2 RETURN rules before DNAT in nat PREROUTING
- `rbtdrc_sentry_config_postrouting_masquerade` ✓ — asserted entry-port MASQUERADE present in nat POSTROUTING
- `rbtdrc_sentry_config_forward_estab_related` ✓ — asserted ESTABLISHED,RELATED ACCEPT on FORWARD chain before `-j RBM-FORWARD` jump

And the existing 55 cases still pass — no regression introduced by the additions.

## State

Pace ₢A_AAn has three commits:

| Commit | Subject |
|---|---|
| `3594d08e` | The four assertion cases + helper, registered in `RBTDRC_CASES_SECURITY` |
| `833b9149` | Tadmor sentry hallmark refresh (local kludge) |
| `27c48b2f` | Tadmor bottle hallmark refresh (local kludge) |

Tree clean. Integration check satisfied. The pace's deliverable (structural-presence backstops for the load-bearing iptables/sysctl clauses identified by ₢A_AAb) is implemented, exercised, and verified.

Ready to wrap ₢A_AAn when you give the word.

input.params: {"coronet": "A_AAn", "summary": "Added four sentry-config presence-assertion crucible cases backstopping the load-bearing iptables clauses and kernel sysctl identified by ₢A_AAb's iteration arc: rbtdrc_sentry_config_rp_filter (branches on RBRN_ENTRY_MODE — asserts /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/all/rp_filter == 2 when enabled, == 1 when disabled), rbtdrc_sentry_config_prerouting_dnat (skips when disabled; otherwise asserts exactly 2 RETURN rules positioned before the DNAT rule in nat-table PREROUTING), rbtdrc_sentry_config_postrouting_masquerade (skips when disabled; otherwise asserts a MASQUERADE rule with -p tcp + --dport is present in nat-table POSTROUTING, discriminating from the egress MASQUERADE), rbtdrc_sentry_config_forward_estab_related (unconditional; asserts an ACCEPT rule matching RELATED+ESTABLISHED is present on the parent FORWARD chain at a position before the -j RBM-FORWARD jump). Plus rbtdrc_read_sentry_env helper for reading RBRN_* values from sentry's process environment via printenv. Cases register in RBTDRC_CASES_SECURITY (shared by tadmor + moriah fixtures), extending coverage from 54 → 58 security cases on those fixtures. Shape checks only — no env-var substitution into iptables output for failure-message decoration; plain failure messages name what regressed and the probe output goes to a per-case file in the trace dir for forensics. Correction relative to the docket prose: FORWARD case targets the parent FORWARD chain rather than RBM-FORWARD as the docket said, because rbjs_sentry.sh installs the ESTABLISHED,RELATED ACCEPT on FORWARD at line 100 before the chain-jump to RBM-FORWARD at line 111 (the script's own comment at lines 166-167 codifies this as load-bearing). Same regression class is caught — the docket's intent (catch removal/repositioning of the return-path baseline) is preserved at the right chain. Integration verification: full tadmor fixture 59/59 PASS against a live charged crucible (54 existing security cases + 4 new + 1 pentacle infra case; sortie cases including direct_sentry_probe, net_dnat_entry_reflection, net_srcip_spoof all green). Tadmor runs entry-mode=enabled, so all four new cases exercised their full assertion paths rather than skipping. The cases backstop regressions to rbjs_sentry.sh's iptables/sysctl shape — fast structural reads that fail loudly before the slower behavioral Ifrit suite has to run. They are early-warning gates, not duplicating behavioral coverage. Pace closed with three commits affiliated: 3594d08e (theurge cases + helper + RBTDRC_CASES_SECURITY registration), 833b9149 (tadmor sentry hallmark refresh — local kludge for this machine; prior hallmark was from cross-platform validation work not in local cache), 27c48b2f (tadmor bottle hallmark refresh — same reason). The two hallmark refreshes were operational prerequisites for the integration check, affiliated with this pace rather than the heat per the precedent set by ₢A_AAc's bottle-hallmark refresh."}
